reviews.review_title: Fun Game To Learn Playing In VR
reviews.review_description:
The Play is a straight forward one of flying thru an active 3D environment picking up energy tokens and upgrade tokens. I’ve played it everyday for a month. Sometimes ya want complicated challenging games to pass the time and others call for fun little enjoyments. This is one of he fun little ones. 
I rate it 4 ½ stars. 👍

It’s and interesting mix of a relaxing flight across space with moments requiring quick reactions. It is not difficult, it’s a nice trip, graphic is pleasant and the game enough interesting to be enjoyable. I like it:)
Not fun. All levels are too similar. All looks the same.

This game has 15 levels that seem to take about 90 seconds to 3 minutes a piece to complete. After completing the first 5 levels you can upgrade to the "best" ship and use it for the rest of the game. I fully upgraded this ship by the time I was half way through.

The game is pretty. That is its primary virtue.

The glaring issue I have with this game, and the reason I gave it one star, is the controls. The game requires you to do all menu interaction and ship navigation with your neck. The Oculus GO controller is only used to "Press and Hold" to make selections. All pointing and flying is done by turning your head. This is horrible on your neck even for a short game and a huge pet peeve of mine. Turning your head should let you see the VR world, not control an avatar or make selections on a menu.

If the game had an alternate control method, I would have given it 3 or 4 stars. A short game is fine. A neck breaker is not.
Relaxing and fun game. Very easy to play.

The 3d effects are great but the tap to hold mechanic is very annoying and since the location if these tabs are low it also strains the neck. Just use a simple tap on the touchpad for activation, like evetyone else does. Seriously this simple thing reduced my opinion of this game.

developer_response.body:
Hi, Homer! 
You definitely right and we added this piece of functionality to our backlog. Follow SpaceMaze updates and thanks for your suggestion!

reviews.review_description: need to add fire and colectebles items, and levels ... one more endless mode
Great title to pick up for your oculus
Beautiful graphics. Running very smooth. One of my favorites

developer_response.body: Thanks for you nice appreciation, CK. We tried hard for making you immerse into smooth and enjoyable gameplay.

reviews.review_description: I checked the previews and reviews.  Looked like a boring game but it is actually quiet fun.  Simple - well done.  Relaxing - not extremely challenging but lotsa fun.  The graphics and super and the controls are really good.  Nice liitle one
I really love this it keeps me busy
This is a pretty fun little game, certainly worth a dollar or so, but it is nowhere near as engaging, immersive or challenging as the Anshar games. Its kind of like Anshar lite, or Anshar training

I love this game! I'm new to oculus and gaming in general, so I expect to dislike most games. But this one was so fun that I finished all levels in one sitting - took me about 2 hours.
I wish there were more levels but it is great and well worth a try!
I'm addicted and must play this daily.  Never mind getting a bit of unexpected cardio from ducking and moving.  It's straight forward collecting and flying.  Great for learning VR.

reviews.review_title: Easily one of my favorites on OGo
reviews.review_description: Good intro levels to get used to the controls, quick progression through models and upgrades, and both relaxing and challenging in a fun way.  Somewhat short (if you don't care about perfect runs), but fair for the price and worthy of level replays.
It looks like one of the coolest games I have ever heard of and fun!!
